response:
Your heart pumps blood through a complex series and coordinated action of contraction and relaxation cycles. It has four chamber,
the right ventricle, left ventricle and the two atria.
The ventricles contract, whichelling the heart to pump blood out of the right ventricle to pulmonary arteries, while when the contracts, the blood flows into two arteries; the artery that delivers Oxygen-rich it into the organs in the system, and the veins that carries carbon dioxide-rich blood back to the lungs to be re-porbed to the heart. This process repeats.

The cause is usually due to the movement of the tectonic plates, two large large fixed solid plates of the Earth's outer crust. These occur when they plates either away from the move past each move towards. The movement of plates causes friction or stress on the Earth's crust. Eventually, the stress becomes too much and the crust gives way, causing the crust to shake suddenly. The shaking is then felt as earthquakes.
